titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Wilfrid Spender to Lady Lillian Spender, 22 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Bliss Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Sir Wilfrid Bliss Spender (1876-1960) to his wife, Lady Lillian Spender, née Dean, (1880-1968). In this brief letter Spender informs Lillian that his brother Rex has arrived. He also informs her that he is being made a General Staff Officer in the 4th Army, and will not protest the appointment, although he will regret leaving his brother and the Ulstermen.Sir Wilfrid Spender was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender wrote a collection of letters to his wife Lady Lillian Spender. The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War. Lady Lillian served in the Ulster Volunteer Nursing Corps during the war, and was an organiser of the Ulster Division Comforts Fund.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
July 22 My pr Rex has come & I have
seen him & we dined
together. Please heaven to
look after him till the
end of the war. My address is
capt. Lt Col WBS
Gen' Staff
4 Army
I got the win that I
was appointed GSO 4 Army today
& with very mixed feelings.
If I had not had that view
I might have declined to have the Division but
that has decided me that
My work is done here,
but you know that
it means to leave Rex
& my own Ulstermen Your b w WB Spender I told Rex you would
undertake commissions
for him if necessary.
Please send out his
fruit cake etc as soon
as possible
